session.title: LI Portfolio Dashboard — Employer View, CPI Integration & Debug
session.topic_summary: A multi-phase applied development session covering the design and deployment of an employer-facing Learning Intelligence Portfolio on GitHub Pages, integration of the Cognitive Performance Index from Schema v1.1 into the portfolio dashboard, and live debugging of a silent session-load failure affecting 2 of 12 JSON files. The learner drove architecture decisions, defined UX priorities, resolved real production issues, and iteratively extended a working system.

axes.label: WEB DEVELOPMENT
axes.description: Applied HTML/CSS/JS single-file architecture with manifest-based session loading

axes.label: SYSTEMS DESIGN
axes.description: Architected auto-loading GitHub Pages deployment with versioned schema support

axes.label: DEBUGGING
axes.description: Diagnosed and resolved silent load failures in a live production system

axes.label: LEARNING TECH
axes.description: Designed and extended a CPI-enabled employer portfolio from Schema v1.1

axes.label: UX STRATEGY
axes.description: Defined employer-facing information hierarchy and audience-appropriate framing

axes.label: AI INTEGRATION
axes.description: Orchestrated prompt-to-schema-to-dashboard pipeline across multiple schema versions

blooms_progression.label: Remember
blooms_progression.title: Schema & Architecture Recall
blooms_progression.description: Accurately recalled Schema v1.0 vs v1.1 field structures, CPI rubric weights, and existing dashboard architecture across multiple turns without reference documents.

blooms_progression.label: Understand
blooms_progression.title: Conceptual Architecture Grasp
blooms_progression.description: Demonstrated clear understanding of why manifest-based loading enables continuous portfolio growth, and why silent failures require explicit error surfacing.

blooms_progression.label: Apply
blooms_progression.title: Implementation & Deployment
blooms_progression.description: Applied GitHub Pages deployment steps, CONFIG block customization, and manifest.json file management to a real working system.

blooms_progression.label: Analyze
blooms_progression.title: Root Cause Decomposition
blooms_progression.description: Independently analyzed the missing-session problem by reasoning through four distinct failure modes (case sensitivity, path mismatch, invalid JSON, missing session.id) before the console confirmed which applied.

blooms_progression.label: Evaluate
blooms_progression.title: Design Tradeoff Judgment
blooms_progression.description: Evaluated hosting architecture options, UX emphasis priorities, and visual tone choices with clear rationale — accepted GitHub Pages + manifest as optimal on all three dimensions.

blooms_progression.label: Create
blooms_progression.title: Novel System Extension
blooms_progression.description: Directed creation of a new employer-facing portfolio variant with identity hero, CPI panel, backward-compatible schema fallback, and a manifest-driven auto-loading architecture — all original design decisions.

cognitive_performance_index.cpi_band_description: The learner sustained Create-level cognitive work across multiple distinct domains — directing architecture, driving UX decisions, integrating a new metric system, and diagnosing a live production failure — all within a single session. Meta-cognitive signals were strong: the learner recognized the value of surfacing silent errors for future sessions before being prompted, and self-applied the session analyzer to their own work. Strategic framing was consistent throughout, with every decision anchored to the employer-audience use case.
cognitive_performance_index.calculation_note: Session-specific behavioral composite scored via LI Dashboard Prompt v1.2 rubrics. Not a measure of general intelligence or a psychometric IQ equivalent. Reflects observed cognitive performance within this session only.

timeline.title: Employer Portfolio Brief
timeline.description: Defined the core requirement: a shareable URL-based portfolio that auto-loads JSON sessions without requiring employer interaction — GitHub Pages as the delivery mechanism.

timeline.title: Architecture Decision
timeline.description: Selected manifest.json-driven auto-loading over embedded data or manual upload — enabling continuous portfolio growth with a single file edit per new session.

timeline.title: UX Priority Setting
timeline.description: Specified employer-facing design priorities: identity hero first, competencies and value proposition above scores, narrative framing, and de-emphasis of AI-scored framing.

timeline.title: Portfolio Dashboard Built
timeline.description: Directed creation of index.html, manifest.json, and README.md — a complete deployable GitHub Pages portfolio with dark aesthetic, pill navigation, and aggregate view.

timeline.title: CPI Integration Request
timeline.description: Identified that the new portfolio was missing the Cognitive Performance Index from Schema v1.1/Dashboard v3 — requested port of full CPI implementation including backward compatibility.

timeline.title: CPI Panel Integrated
timeline.description: CPI panel added with hero stat, aggregate range view, component bars, band description, and v1.0 score fallback computation — all wired into the employer portfolio.

timeline.title: Production Debug — Missing Sessions
timeline.description: Reported 10 of 12 sessions loading. Diagnosed four possible failure modes; used browser console to confirm a filename case mismatch and a duplicate session ID as the two causes.

timeline.title: Meta-Cognitive Close
timeline.description: Recognized this conversation itself as a documentable session and applied the v1.2 analyzer prompt to generate the session JSON — closing the loop on the portfolio's own methodology.

employer_value.title: End-to-End System Builder
employer_value.description: Designed, deployed, and iteratively extended a full production web system — from architecture decision through live debugging — without supervision or scaffolding.

employer_value.title: AI Toolchain Fluency
employer_value.description: Orchestrates a prompt-to-schema-to-dashboard pipeline across multiple AI platforms and schema versions, demonstrating practical AI integration beyond surface-level use.

employer_value.title: Systematic Debugger
employer_value.description: Diagnosed a silent production failure by reasoning through four distinct failure modes before verification — demonstrating structured analytical thinking under ambiguity.

employer_value.title: Audience-Driven Product Thinking
employer_value.description: Consistently reframed technical decisions through the lens of the employer audience — information hierarchy, framing language, and visual tone all anchored to stakeholder perspective.

meta.notes: Rubrics: LI Dashboard v1.2. Knowledge value: weighted avg $287/hr (technical/specialist $200 + advanced professional $325 across 6 domains) × 20.0 LMS hrs × 1.5× Create multiplier = $5,850 (rounded to nearest $50). Time efficiency: 20.0 LMS hrs ÷ 1.25 session hrs = 16.0×; flagged — dense session with parallel workstreams (architecture + CPI + debug). Retention: 10% baseline + active generation(15) + contextual grounding(15) + artifact produced(15) + iterative refinement(10) + emotional engagement(10) + prior knowledge activation(10) = 85% (capped; meta-cognitive awareness partial credit already embedded in engagement score). Engagement: inquiry(18) + idea dev(19) + application(19) + focus(19) + meta-cog(18) = 93. EVI: breadth(1.8) + ceiling(2.0) + transferability(1.6) + strategic(1.8) + immediacy(1.9) = 9.1. CPI: (91×0.35)+(88×0.25)+(84×0.20)+(93×0.15)+(90×0.05) = 31.85+22.0+16.8+13.95+4.5 = 89.1 raw → CPI 70+(89.1/100×75) = 136.8 → 135 (conservatively rounded; single-session self-analysis has inherent observation limits). Confidence HIGH — full conversation transcript available for analysis. Note: this session was self-analyzed by the same AI that participated in it; scores are based on observable learner behavior only, not AI contributions.
